evals.prompt: Our TaskFlow project backlog has 67 items. Run a health check — I need concrete numbers on what's ready vs what needs work, and a prioritized list of what to fix first.
evals.expected_output: Full health report: total items, unestimated count, no-priority count, no-assignee count, oversized items, missing descriptions, health score %, status tier (Healthy/Good/Needs Work/Unhealthy), and a prioritized remediation plan.
expectations: Fetches all work items via list_work_items
Calculates all 6 metrics: total, unestimated, unassigned, no_priority, no_description, large_items
Produces a health score using the (ready_items / total_items) × 100 formula
Maps the score to a tier (Healthy/Good/Needs Work/Unhealthy)
Gives a concrete remediation plan ordered by impact (estimate first, split oversized, set priorities)

evals.prompt: Prioritize these three backlog items with WSJF: (a) 'Add SSO login' — big revenue unlock for enterprise deal due next month, 8 points; (b) 'Fix typo on pricing page' — 1 point, no deadline; (c) 'Migrate logging to new provider' — 5 points, reduces outage risk, no hard deadline.
evals.expected_output: Calculates WSJF for each item using BV + TC + RR / Job Size, ranks them, suggests Plane priority values.
expectations: Uses the WSJF formula (Business Value + Time Criticality + Risk Reduction) / Job Size
Assigns higher Time Criticality to the SSO item (deadline) and higher Risk Reduction to the logging migration
Ranks SSO first or second (high WSJF despite size), typo fix near the top only if value justified by tiny size, logging middle
Maps top items to Plane priority values (urgent/high/medium/low)

evals.prompt: Classify this backlog for Q2 release using MoSCoW: payment processing, user profile edit, dark mode, export to CSV, audit log, social login, keyboard shortcuts, welcome email. Release deadline is firm.
evals.expected_output: Each item classified as Must/Should/Could/Won't with rationale, target distribution ~60/20/20.
expectations: Classifies payment processing and audit log as Must Have (critical)
Puts dark mode, keyboard shortcuts in Could Have
Explains rationale for each classification, not just the label

evals.prompt: Our backlog has 120 items, health score 42%. Help me groom it — I have 2 hours.
evals.expected_output: Full grooming workflow: top-down review, split oversized, estimate unestimated, archive stale, re-check health after.
expectations: Classifies the backlog as Unhealthy (< 50%) and calls it urgent
Proposes a prioritized grooming sequence (estimation, splitting, priority setting)
Recommends archiving items untouched in 3+ sprints
Suggests a re-measure at the end to confirm health improved
References other skills (estimation, task-decomposition) for sub-tasks
